_comment: Public clock reference data. Ratios are derivable from public MAME driver XTAL literals; the ref-swap and pixel-PLL pattern from the public open-fpga/core-template + agg23/analogue-pocket-utils. Core clock ratios are preserved verbatim in a port (dictated by reused game RTL); the universal transforms are the 50->74.25 reference swap and adding an explicit pixel-clock PLL output.

ref_swap.note: MiSTer assumes a 50 MHz board reference; the Pocket supplies 74.25 MHz on clk_74a. Re-target the PLL reference input to 74.25 MHz and re-solve the multipliers to keep every core clock ratio identical.

pixel_pll.note: Where MiSTer drove pixels with a ce_pix clock-enable, add an explicit pixel-clock PLL output (= core clock / N) feeding video_rgb_clock; the APF scaler wants a real pixel clock, not a clock-enable.
pixel_pll.provenance: public open-fpga/core-template video path (video_rgb_clock) + observed in public ports.

families.core_ratio: 3:1 (sys 32 MHz : mem 96 MHz), pixel 8 MHz explicit
families.note: Irem M72: master XTAL 32 MHz, SDRAM at 3x, pixel = master/4 = 8 MHz. One PLL; add clk_vid.

families.core_ratio: 3:1 (sys 40 MHz : mem 120 MHz), pixel 6.667 MHz explicit
families.note: Irem M92: pixel 6.667 = 26.6667 MHz XTAL / 4 (exact); the 40 MHz sys is a SYNTHETIC jtframe base (6x pixel), not a raw XTAL -- flag as synthetic. Split to core_pll + sdram_pll.
families.provenance: mamedev/mame src/mame/irem/m92.cpp (26.6667 MHz XTAL); public opengateware/arcade-irem_m92.

families.note: Genesis: NTSC master 53.693175 MHz IS the sys clock; SDRAM 2x; pixel ~= master/8. Direct from XTAL.
families.provenance: mamedev/mame src/mame/sega/megadriv.cpp XTAL 53.693175 MHz; public agg23/openFPGA-Genesis.

families.core_ratio: 4:1 (sys 21.477 MHz : mem 85.9 MHz), pixel 5.369 MHz explicit
families.note: NES: RP2A03 NTSC XTAL 21.477272 MHz IS the sys clock; SDRAM 4x; pixel = master/4 (PPU dot clock). PAL retune disabled in the port.

families.core_ratio: 16:4:1 (sys 21.48 MHz : mem 85.9 MHz : pixel 5.37 MHz); separate 24.576 MHz audio domain
families.note: SNES: MCLK NTSC 21.477272 MHz; SDRAM 4x; pixel = master/4; the S-SMP audio runs on a SEPARATE 24.576 MHz domain (24.576/12 = 2.048 MHz) -- a genuine second clock domain to plan.
families.provenance: mamedev/mame src/mame/nintendo/snes.cpp (MCLK 21.477272 MHz, audio 24.576 MHz); public agg23/openfpga-SNES.
